title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
An iterator is any object with a .next() method that returns { value, done }. An iterable is any object with a [Symbol.iterator]() method that returns an iterator.

Built-in iterables: arrays, strings, maps, sets, NodeLists, arguments, generators.

The for...of loop, spread operator, and destructuring all work on any iterable by calling [Symbol.iterator]().

You can make any object iterable by implementing [Symbol.iterator]. This is the protocol that powers generators and many ES6 features.
